pool.baseline: The candles have all guttered out, leaving only the bitter scent of wax and the hollow ache in my chest. He is gone... and with him, every light that once graced this wretched existence. I look at the moon and see only a cold, indifferent eye watching my ruin. There is no poetry left in living, only the slow, agonizing rhythm of a heart that refuses to stop beating when it has nothing left to beat for. I feel as though I am already a ghost, drifting through these darkened halls, waiting for the shadows to finally claim what is left of me.
